meaning: Put an end to STH (relationship, discussion, talks, negotiations)
sentence: Following a disagreement, the government broke off diplomatic relations with China.

meaning: Surrender control or responsibility for STH/SB to SB else, esp. officially
sentence: The government isn’t willing to hand over power to local authorities.

meaning: Collapse, fail; stop functioning properly (heart, knees)
sentence: At 95 years of age, her heart finally gave out.

meaning: Invest or devote so as to achieve STH (time, effort, work)
sentence: You need to put in more hours at the office every day.

meaning: Fully understand or grasp the meaning of STH
sentence: You have to explain more thoroughly; it’s too difficult to take in.

meaning: Deliberately take no action/remain passive about STH
sentence: We won’t just sit back and watch the situation getting worse and worse.

meaning: Decide not to do or say STH
sentence: They should not hold back from joining us if they want to.

meaning: Move to a better position; advance to a higher level/rank
sentence: She moved up from secretary to senior manager in just a few years.

meaning: ( + with …) Continue doing STH after stopping; proceed with STH
sentence: We might as well get on with it if we want to finish on time.

meaning: Appear or arrive; come into existence
sentence: I didn’t expect such an opportunity to come along.

meaning: Distinguish oneself/itself by being better, more significant or more impressive than other people/things
sentence: Excellent product quality is what made the brand stand out from its competitors.

meaning: Withdraw or retreat from an activity or location, esp. military
sentence: The army was forced to pull back due to bad weather.

meaning: Use STH (or become used) completely so that nothing is left
sentence: Unfortunately we’ve run out of biscuits.

meaning: Surrender possession or control to SB/STH (esp. in authority)
sentence: The policeman turned over the criminal to the jail guard.

meaning: Present or stage (play, show, competition)
sentence: They put on such an incredible show last night!

meaning: Regain possession or control over STH
sentence: The politician’s ultimate goal is to take back the Senate.

meaning: See or hear with difficulty
sentence: I could barely make out his face in the dark.

meaning: Pay back the effort spent in doing STH by becoming profitable or effective
sentence: All the hard work will pay off in the end.

meaning: (Get off to a … start) Begin STH in a certain way
sentence: The team has got off to a good start this season.

meaning: Arrive or make an appearance somewhere
sentence: He turned up to the meeting half an hour late.

meaning: (+ to …) Begin to pay serious attention to STH
sentence: We should get down to discussing those issues as soon as possible.

meaning: Interrupt SB as they are speaking
sentence: The teacher cut off the student in the middle of her sentence.

meaning: Ask SB to do a particular job or task
sentence: He had been brought in to save the company.

meaning: Make STH known or accessible to the public (information, products)
sentence: Police have put out a warning about thieves in the area.

meaning: Make STH free from dangerous, unacceptable or controversial activities or contents
sentence: He was asked to clean up his language during the interview.

meaning: Explain or present STH clearly, esp. officially and in writing
sentence: The official recommendations were set out in the document.

meaning: Divide or separate into categories or smaller components so as to make it easier to understand or deal with
sentence: Let’s break down the task into three easy steps.

meaning: Make an effort to address or communicate with SB, so as to help them or involve them in STH
sentence: The government has recently been trying to reach out to right-wing voters.

meaning: Move, progress or increase at the same rate or pace as SB/STH
sentence: Workers’ income has not kept up with inflation.

meaning: (+ with …) Be willing to accept STH unpleasant or not desirable; tolerate
sentence: I won’t put up with your bad behaviour for much longer.

meaning: Make STH become better or more successful than it previously was (economy, business)
sentence: People have stopped believing the President could turn around the economy.

meaning: Establish as valid or genuine
sentence: You have to back up your accusations with solid evidence.

meaning: Take care of SB and make sure they are well; protect SB's interests
sentence: We look out for each other as if we were family.

meaning: Obtain an official document or service from an authority
sentence: I had to take out a loan to cover all my expenses.

meaning: Look at STH/SB again after having momentarily looked elsewhere
sentence: Sometimes we look back on those days and realize we had a very happy life.

meaning: Become higher in value; increase
sentence: Oil prices have gone up last year.

meaning: Decrease in value or amount
sentence: I don’t think prices will go down.

meaning: Said to show SB disbelief, disagreement, or anger
sentence: Oh come on, you’re just lying to me!

meaning: Become involved in a situation
sentence: We need experts to come in and give us advice.

meaning: Become known or revealed after being kept secret
sentence: The news came out that he was leaving the team.

meaning: Cause to happen or emerge, especially something positive
sentence: This government decision will bring about many important changes.

meaning: Conduct, perform, or complete a task, plan, study, or duty
sentence: The researchers carried out the survey in three schools.

meaning: Exclude something as impossible or unsuitable
sentence: The evidence ruled out a simple explanation for the results.

meaning: Direct attention to a fact, idea, or piece of information
sentence: The author pointed out several problems with the earlier study.

meaning: Summarize the main points of something
sentence: The final paragraph sums up the main findings of the study.

meaning: Take further action after something has started or been reported
sentence: The team followed up the interview with a short questionnaire.

meaning: Present or arrange information clearly
sentence: The introduction lays out the main questions of the paper.

meaning: Find, calculate, or understand a solution
sentence: The students worked out the answer after comparing the two graphs.
